Human Species & Prehistory.description: Out of Africa, mastery of fire; Asia, Europe.
Likely common ancestor of Neanderthals and Sapiens; Europe, Africa.
Europe, Middle East; Mousterian tools, burials.
Our species — appeared in Africa, worldwide expansion, art, symbols.

Human Species.description: Emblematic fossil discovered in Ethiopia — biped, one of the best-documented ancestors of the human lineage.
First migrations out of the African continent — progressive settlement of Asia then Europe.
First anatomically modern humans, in North Africa (Morocco, Jebel Irhoud).
Migration of Homo sapiens out of Africa and gradual peopling of the entire world.
Extinction of Neanderthal Man, probably through contact and competition with Homo sapiens.

Culture & Society.description: Pebble tools fashioned in East Africa — birth of human material culture.
Homo erectus controls fire — cooking food, warmth, protection, social bond.
Decorated caves (Chauvet, Lascaux) — creative explosion attesting to the abstract thought of Homo sapiens.
First domesticated animal — alliance between Homo sapiens and the grey wolf.
Agriculture and animal husbandry — settlement of populations, birth of permanent villages.

Culture & Society.titre: First Cities and Invention of Writing
Culture & Society.description: Uruk (Mesopotamia) — first large cities, administration and cuneiform writing.

Time Markers.description: Reference point of the Gregorian calendar.
